seoTitle: Create Journal Entries in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: How to record balanced double-entry journal entries in QuickBooks Online for manual corrections, year-end adjustments, and recurring entries.
intro: Manual corrections, year-end adjustments, and inter-company transfers all rely on traditional double-entry bookkeeping. QuickBooks Online's Journal Entry form lets you record parallel debit and credit rows directly, with a clear audit trail. This guide walks through structuring a balanced entry and even automating recurring ones.

steps.heading: Open the Journal Entry form
steps.body: Click + New, then select Journal Entry under the Other column.

steps.heading: Set the date and reference
steps.body: Enter the transaction date and establish a unique, sequential journal number in the header row.

steps.heading: Enter your first line
steps.body: Select an account and enter the amount under the Debits column for the first row.

steps.heading: Balance it with a second line
steps.body: Select your offsetting account on the next row and enter the matching amount under the Credits column.

steps.heading: Add a memo and save
steps.body: Write a clear description for the audit trail, optionally set it as a recurring transaction, then click Save and Close.

faq.question: What happens if my debits and credits don't match?
faq.answer: QuickBooks won't let the entry balance properly, so you'll need to adjust the amounts until both columns are equal.

faq.question: Can I assign a journal entry line to a specific customer or vendor?
faq.answer: Yes, the Name column on each row lets you tag a line to a specific customer or vendor profile for tracking purposes.

faq.question: How do I automate a recurring journal entry?
faq.answer: Use the Make Recurring option at the bottom of the form to turn the entry into an automated repeating template.

faq.question: How can I verify my journal entry posted correctly?
faq.answer: Run a General Ledger report from the Reports panel to confirm the entry appears with the correct debit and credit amounts.
